metadata.title: Arcline Platform — Integration Guide
metadata.author: Arcline Engineering
metadata.description: Technical reference for integrating external systems with the Arcline Platform API

props.text: Confidential — Arcline Inc. — Do not distribute
Last updated: April 2026  |  Audience: Developers, System Architects, DevOps Engineers

This guide covers the integration of external systems with the Arcline Platform API. It is intended for developers and system architects building custom integrations, data pipelines, and embedded analytics solutions. The API follows RESTful conventions, uses OAuth 2.0 for authentication, and returns JSON responses.

items: Arcline account with API access enabled (Settings > API Keys > Generate)
Node.js 20+ or Python 3.11+ (official SDKs available for both)
Network access to api.arcline.io on port 443 (TLS 1.3 required)
Familiarity with REST APIs, OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow, and webhook architectures

props.text: The Arcline Platform exposes a RESTful API organized around resources. All endpoints accept and return JSON. Authentication uses OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens with configurable scopes. Rate limiting is applied per-token with configurable burst allowances.

props.caption: Figure 1: High-level architecture — API gateway, authentication layer, microservices, and data stores

props.text: Install the SDK, configure your credentials, and make your first API call:
All API requests require a valid bearer token. Tokens are obtained via the OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow and expire after 3600 seconds (1 hour). The SDK handles token refresh automatically.

POST https://auth.arcline.io/oauth/token
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

grant_type=client_credentials
&client_id=YOUR_CLIENT_ID
&client_secret=YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET
&scope=read:data write:data
Include the token in subsequent requests via the Authorization header:

items: Never embed client secrets in client-side code or version control
Use environment variables or a secrets manager (AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault)
Rotate client secrets every 90 days — the API supports two active secrets for zero-downtime rotation
Request only the scopes your integration needs (principle of least privilege)
Monitor the /oauth/audit endpoint for unauthorized token requests

props.text: All list endpoints support cursor-based pagination. The response includes a meta object with pagination details:
Pass the next_cursor value as the ?cursor query parameter to fetch the next page. Maximum limit is 100 items per request.

API requests are rate-limited per token. Exceeding the limit returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests. Use the Retry-After header (in seconds) to determine when to retry. The SDK handles retries with exponential backoff automatically.

Webhooks deliver real-time event notifications to your endpoint via HTTP POST. Configure webhooks in the dashboard (Settings > Webhooks) or via the /webhooks API. All payloads are signed with HMAC-SHA256 using your webhook secret for verification.

cells.content: New user signup or SSO provision

props.text: Every webhook delivery includes the following envelope:
Verify the X-Arcline-Signature header to ensure the payload was sent by Arcline and has not been tampered with:
Failed deliveries (non-2xx response or timeout after 30 seconds) are retried with exponential backoff:

After 5 failed attempts, the event is marked as failed and visible in the dashboard. Webhooks with > 95% failure rate over 48 hours are automatically disabled with an email notification to the account owner.
The API uses standard HTTP status codes. All error responses include a JSON body with code, message, request_id, and optional details fields.

cells.content: Malformed JSON or invalid query parameters
Resource does not exist or was deleted
Valid JSON but semantic validation failed
Rate limit exceeded — check Retry-After header
Unexpected server error — include request_id in support tickets

props.text: For POST and PATCH requests, include an Idempotency-Key header to safely retry failed requests without creating duplicates:

POST /v3/users
Idempotency-Key: idk_unique-request-id-12345
Content-Type: application/json

{ "email": "jane@example.com", "name": "Jane Doe" }
Idempotency keys are valid for 24 hours. Replayed requests within this window return the original response without executing the operation again.

cells.content: Refresh token; verify client_id/secret; check clock skew < 60s
Check dashboard > Webhooks > Delivery Log; re-enable if disabled
Use ?limit to reduce page size; add ?fields to select specific columns
Re-issue token with required scopes; check admin:users for user data
Add your domain in Settings > API Keys > Allowed Origins

props.text: Enable verbose logging in the SDK to inspect request/response details:
